resources.string: Cleanify uses Accessibility to automate navigating Android app details in order to stop selected apps. Use with care.
To stop apps on an unrooted device, Cleanify must be enabled as an Accessibility Service. Turn it on in Android Accessibility settings, then come back.

string-array.name: no_running_apps_happy_messages
string-array.item: All quiet in the background — enjoy the calm.
Nothing running back there. Your phone gets a little breather.
Background queue is empty. Nice and tidy.
No stray apps today: you’re in great shape.
Zero background buzz. That’s a small win worth noticing.
Clear skies in the background. You’re all set.
Peace and quiet: no apps fighting for attention right now.
Your background is happily empty. Savor the moment.
Nothing to clean here: everything’s already taking a nap.
A calm background is a happy background.

resources.string: No apps are marked as stopped yet.
Finding which apps are stopped vs running…

string-array.name: dashboard_loading_messages
string-array.item: Indexing package states—almost there.
Please wait; this keeps your lists accurate.
Querying the system for force-stopped apps…
Patience: Android doesn’t expose this instantly.
Reconciling background vs stopped processes…
Still working: good things take a second.
Scanning application flags (FLAG_STOPPED)…
Hold on: we’re matching reality to your list.
Syncing runtime state with storage…
Almost done—thanks for waiting.

resources.string: Quiet the noise in your background
Add apps you are okay force-stopping when you need focus or battery.
Tip: select running apps, then tap the bolt in the toolbar to force clean only those, or the remove icon to drop apps you cannot kill (e.g. Bluetooth, Files). Clean Up stops every running app.
Remove selected apps from your list
